creator: freebsd-2024@virtual-estates.net
text:
> this would effective block the port due to security

Don't be ridiculous. The exploit is merely of a denial of service kind, and IS NOT REMOTE. It is not any more dangerous -- indeed, it is far LESS dangerous -- that /bin/sh, that's susceptible to a forkbomb.

You're not suggesting, we "deprecate and set expiration date" for sh, are you?
